title: cors() configured with origin: "*" — any website can call the API with the user's session

params.passDetail: cors() sets an explicit allow-list origin (not "*") — cross-origin access is restricted to trusted sites.
params.failDetail: cors() is called with origin: "*", allowing ANY website to make cross-origin requests to this API. Combined with credentials, this lets a malicious page act as the logged-in user. Set origin to an explicit allow-list (a string, array, or function), not "*".
params.absentCallDetail: No cors() call in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: cors() does not pass an origin literal; verify the resolved origin is a trusted allow-list, not "*".

provenance.note: origin: "*" is the most common cors misconfiguration; with credentials it enables any site to ride a user's authenticated session. AI generators often emit it to 'fix' CORS errors during development.
